person.name: Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale

nameForms.fullText: Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Sir John de Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn & Eskdale (1278-1337) was a 13th-14th century Scottish noble.

John, born in 1278, was the son of Nicholas de Graham of Dalkeith and Abercorn and Mary de Strathearn.

He fought at the Battle of Bannockburn against the English on 23/24 June 1314 and as a result had his Northumberland estates confiscated. King Edward II of England denounced John as an enemy and rebel, and granted his Scottish lands to Hugh le Despenser. He signed the Declaration of Arbroath in 1320.

John died on 25 April 1337.

«b»Family and issue«/b»
John married Isabella, and had the following known issue:

1.) Sir John de Graham, last of Dalkeith, Abercorn, and Eskdale, died without issue; resigned Dalkeith in favor of William Douglas of Laudonia 6 Jan 1343

2.) Sybilla de Graham, married Reginald de Mure, had issue. Abercorn passed to the Mure family.

3.) Isabel de Graham, married Walter Stewart, 6th High Steward of Scotland as his second wife, had issue.

4.) Margaret de Graham, married William Douglas, Lord of Liddesdale as his 1st wife.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale於大約1278年出生在蘇格蘭Linlithgowshire阿爾可時，他的父親Nicholas de Graham38歲，母親Mary Strathearn31歲。他於大約1288年與Isabella Kirkpatrick在蘇格蘭Linlithgowshire阿爾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1337年4月25日在他的家鄉享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Graham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, recorded in Domesday Book as Graham (as well as Grantham, Grandham, and Granham). See also Grantham .

Irish (especially Ulster): in Ireland Graham is generally the Scottish name, but it has additionally been adopted as an Anglicized form of both Ó Creacháin (see Crehan ) and Ó Gormghaile (see Gormley ).

History: The surname Graham was taken to Scotland at the beginning of the 12th century by the Norman baron William de Graham, holder of the manor of Grantham in Lincolnshire, from whom many if not all modern bearers are probably descended. The chief of Clan Graham is the Duke of Montrose.

variants.name: Greim
meanings: South German: from a short form of a compound personal name beginning with the element grīm ‘mask, helmet’.

variants.name: Grahm
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire and northern England): shortened form of   Graham  .

variants.name: Grantham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire and East Yorkshire): habitational name from Grantham in Lincolnshire, of uncertain origin. The final element is Old English hām ‘homestead’; the first may be Old English grand ‘gravel’ or perhaps a personal name Granta, which probably originated as a byname meaning ‘snarler’. See also Graham .

variants.name: Grahame
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Graham  . — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 261, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale was born about 1278, in Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland, his father, Nicholas de Graham, was 38 and his mother, Mary Strathearn, was 31. He married Isabella Kirkpatrick about 1288, in Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 5 daughters. He died on 25 April 1337, in his hometown, at the age of 60.

Il a épousé Isabella Kirkpatrick environ 1288, à Abercorn, Linlithgowshire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 25 avril 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdaleが約1278年にスコットランドLinlithgowshireアバーコルンで生まれた時，父Nicholas de Grahamは38歳，母Mary Strathearnは31歳であった。約1288年にスコットランドLinlithgowshireアバーコルンでIsabella Kirkpatrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1337年4月25日に故郷で60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale(이)가 대략 1278년 스컷랜드 Linlithgowshire 압르코른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Nicholas de Graham(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Strathearn(은)는 31세였다. 그는 대략 1288년 스컷랜드 Linlithgowshire 압르코른에서 Isabella Kirkpatrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1337년 4월 25일 그의 고향에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Graham of Dalkeith, Abercorn and Eskdale родился около 1278 г. в местности абиркорн, Linlithgowshire, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Nicholas de Graham было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Strathearn, 31. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabella Kirkpatrick около 1288 г. в местности абиркорн, Linlithgowshire, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 5 дочерей. Он умер 25 апреля 1337 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 60 лет.
